pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh briefing text: 'Analyst Role: Logistics Planning Analyst'
Contextual verification from setup_task.sh briefing (barge traffic, draft restrictions, waterway logistics)
setup_task.sh DATA_FILE variable and environment install scripts (/home/ga/PanoplyData/prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc)
Inspection of the downloaded real netCDF file and setup_task.sh requirements
Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh OUTPUT_DIR variable
Required deliverables section in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh logic

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real, non-synthetic NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1 netCDF precipitation dataset downloaded from NOAA PSL (prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc). Independent python analysis of the target Midwest coordinates (25N to 50N, 105W to 80W) confirms that 67% of grid cells in the region have a positive difference when subtracting September precipitation from May precipitation. The median difference is +0.83 mm/day, successfully validating the verifier's expectation that May is wetter than September visually. The required output paths and specific variable 'prate' are all correctly matched in the setup and export scripts, and the role is explicitly detailed in the briefing as 'Logistics Planning Analyst'.
